_comment: issue M6-09 LogQL-pipeline differential corpus + coverage matrix (e2e/src/logs_corpus.rs + e2e/src/logs.rs). `{R}` in every case's `query` is replaced with this run's run_id at execution time; every case conjoins `run_id="{R}"` plus a `service_name` scope pinning the body shape its parser expects, so a stale stack can never leak foreign streams into a set-equality comparison. Gate discipline (traces precedent, plan v3 delta 5): every stage has >= 1 case, every case starts `mode: "gated"`, and the exclusion list starts EMPTY. A case moves to `mode: "informational"` only via the triage discipline — an observed live divergence, classified fix-our-bug vs ratify-documented-oracle-delta, recorded in docs/benchmarks/logs-differential-ledger.md (the `ledger` field names the entry); PulsusDB stays hard-gated against the corpus expectation even then. `limit` is the query_range request cap on both stores; every case's expected entry set is sized strictly below it (validity gates in logs.rs).

cases.construct: json parser (full flatten, incl. a nested member) + string label filter =
json parser + string label filter =~ (anchored)
logfmt parser + string label filter =
regexp parser (named captures) + string label filter over an extracted label
pattern parser (<name> captures between literals) + string label filter
numeric label filter >= over a plain number
numeric label filter > over a duration literal (unit conversion)
numeric label filter > over a bytes literal (unit conversion)
line_format {{.label}} substitution over parsed labels (rewritten line bodies)
label_format rename (dst=src moves the value, removes the source label)
issue #99: a `| json` over a non-object line errors — the stream carries __error__=JSONParserErr plus the byte-exact __error_details__, compared set-equal against grafana/loki:3.4.2

issue #99: `| __error__ != ""` keeps the errored stream, carrying BOTH __error__ and __error_details__ (the drop side `| __error__ = ""` is a Tier-1 hermetic golden — an empty differential set is not a valid set comparison)
issue #99: `| logfmt | n > 5` fails the numeric conversion — LabelFilterErr plus the byte-exact Go strconv.ParseFloat detail

issue #100: fetch-until-limit ordered-limited case. `| json | status = "503" | took_ms = "500"` is two dropping label filters (fetch_until_limit, scan_limit = limit*factor = 40) whose earliest-limit=4 svc-json survivors (j=9,29,49,69) span >= 2 keyset pages: page 1 (earliest 40 svc-json rows, j in [0,39]) yields only 2 survivors (j=9,29) < 4, forcing a second fetch; page 2 (j in [40,79]) adds j=49,69 to fill at exactly 4. j=9 and j=69 are both GET /api/users 503 500 => the SAME stream carrying two entries, so their arrival order is a real per-stream ordering property. Compared as an ORDERED earliest-4 Vec<(labels,ts,line)> (per-stream ascending verified as received, then k-way merged) against grafana/loki:3.4.2; raw==limit==4 is the page-2 proof. Full tier only (self-gated in run_streams_limited_case).

issue #109: an OTLP log record's InstrumentationScope lands in per-entry structured metadata (Loki 3.4.2 parity, not indexed stream labels). `| scope_name="coll-scope"` is a structured-metadata pipeline filter selecting the single collision-bearing svc-scope witness; the flattened response (last-write-wins collision resolution: dup_key=v_us, scope_name=coll-scope beating scope.name=LOSE, scope_version=1.0, emptyattr="") is compared set-equal PulsusDB==grafana/loki:3.4.2. run_streams_case additionally asserts the {scope_name="coll-scope"} STREAM selector returns empty on both stores (the placement discriminator).
M6-10 client-aggregated count_over_time over a json + label-filter pipeline (instant vector; instant windows are semantically identical on both stores — no tumbling divergence)
M6-10 sum_over_time over unwrap duration(...) with a targeted logfmt extraction (unwrap deletes the unwrapped label; conversion at the unwrap stage)
cases.query: sum_over_time({run_id="{R}", service_name="svc-logfmt"} | logfmt took | unwrap duration(took) [30m])

cases.construct: M6-10 vector aggregation sum by(level) over a client-aggregated count_over_time
M6-10 vector-scalar binary operation over a client-aggregated metric result

M6-10 seeded ledger entry: range-query rate — PulsusDB's epoch-aligned tumbling buckets vs the oracle's request-aligned sliding windows (PulsusDB stays hard-gated against the tumbling by-construction expectation)

M6-10 D1 witness: a GENUINE unwrap conversion failure surviving the pipeline (svc-badunit's took=abc) fails the metric query on BOTH stores — HTTP 400 carrying SampleExtractionErr, never silent exclusion
issue #91 one-to-one on(method) vector matching; output labels are the reduced signature (method)
cases.query: sum by (method) (count_over_time({run_id="{R}", service_name="svc-json"} | json [30m])) / on(method) sum by (method) (count_over_time({run_id="{R}", service_name="svc-json"} | json | status = "200" [30m]))

cases.construct: issue #91 one-to-one ignoring(status) vector matching over by(method) operands
cases.query: sum by (method) (count_over_time({run_id="{R}", service_name="svc-json"} | json [30m])) / ignoring(status) sum by (method) (count_over_time({run_id="{R}", service_name="svc-json"} | json | status = "503" [30m]))

cases.construct: issue #91 many-to-one on(status) group_left; the many (lhs) side {method,status} passes through whole
cases.query: sum by (method, status) (count_over_time({run_id="{R}", service_name="svc-json"} | json [30m])) / on(status) group_left sum by (status) (count_over_time({run_id="{R}", service_name="svc-json"} | json [30m]))

cases.construct: issue #91 one-to-many on(status) group_right; the many (rhs) side {method,status} passes through whole
cases.query: sum by (status) (count_over_time({run_id="{R}", service_name="svc-json"} | json [30m])) * on(status) group_right sum by (method, status) (count_over_time({run_id="{R}", service_name="svc-json"} | json [30m]))

cases.construct: issue #91 on(method) matching on the RANGE path (per-step instant join); informational — same tumbling-vs-sliding window divergence as metric_rate_tumbling

cases.construct: issue #91 ignoring(status) matching on the RANGE path; informational (tumbling-vs-sliding)
cases.query: sum by (method) (count_over_time({run_id="{R}", service_name="svc-json"} | json [1m])) / ignoring(status) sum by (method) (count_over_time({run_id="{R}", service_name="svc-json"} | json | status = "503" [1m]))

cases.construct: issue #91 on(status) group_left matching on the RANGE path; informational (tumbling-vs-sliding)
cases.query: sum by (method, status) (count_over_time({run_id="{R}", service_name="svc-json"} | json [1m])) / on(status) group_left sum by (status) (count_over_time({run_id="{R}", service_name="svc-json"} | json [1m]))

cases.construct: issue #91 on(status) group_right matching on the RANGE path; informational (tumbling-vs-sliding)
cases.query: sum by (status) (count_over_time({run_id="{R}", service_name="svc-json"} | json [1m])) * on(status) group_right sum by (method, status) (count_over_time({run_id="{R}", service_name="svc-json"} | json [1m]))

cases.construct: issue #91 many-to-one WITHOUT group_left/right: both stores reject the query (multiple matches for labels: many-to-one matching must be explicit)
cases.expect_error_substr: multiple matches for labels: many-to-one matching must be explicit
cases.query: sum by (method, status) (count_over_time({run_id="{R}", service_name="svc-json"} | json [30m])) / on(status) sum by (status) (count_over_time({run_id="{R}", service_name="svc-json"} | json [30m]))

cases.construct: issue #91 duplicate one-side signature (many-to-many): both stores reject the query (many-to-many matching not allowed: matching labels must be unique on one side)
cases.expect_error_substr: many-to-many matching not allowed: matching labels must be unique on one side
cases.query: sum by (method, status) (count_over_time({run_id="{R}", service_name="svc-json"} | json [30m])) / on(status) group_left sum by (method, status) (count_over_time({run_id="{R}", service_name="svc-json"} | json [30m]))
